name: 2D lattice basis reduction (Gauss / LLL in 2D)
task: Create /tmp/eval_gauss/gauss.py with function gauss_reduce(b1, b2) where b1 and b2 are 2-tuples of integers (x, y) representing lattice basis vectors. Return a tuple (c1, c2) of 2-tuples-of-integers representing a Gauss-reduced basis of the SAME lattice, satisfying: (a) |c1| <= |c2| (norm), (b) the absolute value of <c1, c2> (dot product) is at most |c1|^2 / 2. Use the iterative Gauss reduction: while |b2| < |b1| swap them; replace b2 with b2 - round(<b1,b2>/<b1,b1>) * b1; repeat until stable. The output basis must span the same lattice as the input (you may verify mentally — we test via the determinant being preserved up to sign).

validation.script:
import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_gauss/gauss.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); gr = mod.gauss_reduce
def norm2(v): return v[0]*v[0] + v[1]*v[1]
def dot(a,b): return a[0]*b[0] + a[1]*b[1]
def det(a,b): return a[0]*b[1] - a[1]*b[0]
def check_reduced(c1, c2, b1, b2):
    n1, n2 = norm2(c1), norm2(c2)
    assert n1 <= n2, f'|c1|>|c2|: {c1},{c2}'
    d = dot(c1, c2)
    assert 2 * abs(d) <= n1, f'Lovasz: 2|<c1,c2>|={2*abs(d)} > |c1|^2={n1}'
    assert abs(det(c1, c2)) == abs(det(b1, b2)), f'lattice changed: det was {det(b1,b2)}, now {det(c1,c2)}'
# Easy cases
for (b1, b2) in [((1,0),(0,1)), ((2,1),(1,2)), ((10,0),(0,10))]:
    c1, c2 = gr(b1, b2); check_reduced(c1, c2, b1, b2)
# Already reduced should stay reduced
c1, c2 = gr((1,0),(0,1)); assert sorted([c1, c2]) == [(0,1),(1,0)] or sorted([c1, c2]) == [(0,-1),(-1,0)] or {tuple(c1), tuple(c2)} == {(1,0),(0,1)}, f'already-reduced changed: {c1},{c2}'
# Skewed bases — must reduce drastically
cases = [((10,0),(7,1)), ((5,3),(7,4)), ((100,1),(99,1)), ((1000,1),(1,1000))]
for (b1, b2) in cases:
    c1, c2 = gr(b1, b2); check_reduced(c1, c2, b1, b2)
    # reduced basis should have small norms relative to input max
    in_max = max(norm2(b1), norm2(b2)); out_max = max(norm2(c1), norm2(c2))
    assert out_max <= in_max, f'reduction did not shrink: {b1},{b2} -> {c1},{c2}'
# Random integer bases
import random; random.seed(31)
for _ in range(20):
    b1 = (random.randint(-50, 50), random.randint(-50, 50))
    b2 = (random.randint(-50, 50), random.randint(-50, 50))
    if det(b1, b2) == 0: continue  # skip degenerate
    c1, c2 = gr(b1, b2); check_reduced(c1, c2, b1, b2)
print('OK')
